sources.name: GWAS Catalog
sources.description: GWAS Catalog records provide publication, association, variant, trait, and study identifiers used by the i-GWASdb component.
sources.notes: Counts describe the GWAS Catalog as of the stated snapshot date and may differ from the live catalog after that date. Although the current GWAS Catalog is mapped to GRCh38.p14, SNP positions stored in the i-GWASdb POS column are reported on GRCh37.

sources.name: Unified Medical Language System® (UMLS®)
sources.description: UMLS Concept Unique Identifiers, preferred concept names, selected public-source definitions, and selected atom metadata support i-CLASSi terminology mapping and disease lookup.

technicalContext.label: Public cache allowlist
technicalContext.value: MSH, NCI, HPO, MEDLINEPLUS, ORPHANET, PDQ

releaseHighlights.description: Norwegian Edition of the Medical Dictionary for Regulatory Activities Terminology (MedDRA)
Slovak Edition of the Medical Dictionary for Regulatory Activities Terminology (MedDRA)
Slovenian Edition of the Medical Dictionary for Regulatory Activities Terminology (MedDRA)

sources.notes: The 2026AA release includes five new translation sources and 81 updated sources, including translation sources and subsources. Public i-CLASSi content excludes Category 3 or otherwise restricted vocabularies.

sources.name: ICD-O-3 Coding Updates
sources.organization: North American Association of Central Cancer Registries (NAACCR)
sources.description: NAACCR ICD-O-3 coding updates provide the ICD-O-3.2 implementation guidelines and numeric and alphabetic coding tables used to review morphology terminology and codes.
sources.notes: The NAACCR page should be consulted for the current implementation guidelines, coding tables, annotated histology list, corrections, and previous-year guidance.

sources.name: ICD-O-4 Coding Updates
sources.organization: International Agency for Research on Cancer (IARC) and World Health Organization (WHO)
sources.description: ICD-O-4 is the fourth edition of the International Classification of Diseases for Oncology. It updates tumour coding structure for topography, morphology or histology, behaviour, and grade, and supports coding of existing and newly defined tumour entities.

technicalContext.label: Primary coding scope
technicalContext.value: Tumour topography, morphology/histology, behaviour, and grade

technicalContext.label: Tumour entity context
technicalContext.value: Developed after review of the 5th edition WHO Classification of Tumours series and intended to support current and future WHO Classification of Tumours content

releaseHighlights.description: Improved structure of unique codes for existing and newly defined tumour entities
Harmonized with the 11th revision of the International Classification of Diseases
Finalized ICD-O-4 topography and morphology tables are expected through the WHO Classification of Tumours Online website
ICD-O-4 book publication by WHO is in preparation

sources.notes: ICD-O-4 is included as a forward-looking source for tracking oncology coding updates. The IARC overview article and WHO Classification of Tumours Online website should be consulted for current content and updates.

sources.name: SEER Hematopoietic and Lymphoid Neoplasm Database
sources.organization: National Cancer Institute, Surveillance, Epidemiology, and End Results Program
sources.usedBy: ICD code conversion and hematopoietic terminology review
sources.description: The SEER Hematopoietic and Lymphoid Neoplasm Database provides disease terminology, ICD-O-3 morphology information, coding guidance, and conversion-oriented ICD code lists.

releaseHighlights.description: Conversion-oriented code list from ICD-O-3 morphology to ICD-9
Conversion-oriented code list from ICD-O-3 morphology to ICD-10
Conversion-oriented code list from ICD-O-3 morphology to ICD-10-CM
Reverse code-list views are also available through the SEER ICD Code Lists interface

sources.notes: Crosswalks support harmonization and review but should not be interpreted as universally exact one-to-one clinical equivalences.

sources.name: Mondo Disease Ontology (MONDO)
sources.usedBy: Disease ontology harmonization and vocabulary mapping
sources.description: Mondo is a disease ontology that aims to harmonize disease definitions across the world. It supports disease concept alignment and cross-resource terminology review for i-CLASSi mapping work.
sources.notes: i-CLASSi records MONDO v2026-06-02 as the disease ontology source snapshot for this release. Consult the Mondo website, GitHub releases, and OBO Foundry page for current ontology content and later releases.

sources.name: Experimental Factor Ontology (EFO)
sources.usedBy: i-GWASdb trait harmonization and vocabulary mapping
sources.description: The Experimental Factor Ontology provides controlled terms for experimental variables, diseases, traits, assays, and related biomedical concepts used in GWAS and data harmonization.

releaseHighlights.description: Added MONDO_1060213 to imports and dependencies
Added assay terms for EFO issue #2707

sources.notes: i-CLASSi records this as the EFO source snapshot for the release. Consult the OLS browser and GitHub release pages for current ontology content and later releases.
